mechanism_narrative: A persistent rise in industrial production (INDPRO) signals expanding aggregate supply and robust corporate earnings, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent month. The mechanism operates continuously: any month-over-month increase in INDPRO is treated as a bullish signal.
failure_condition: Over any 3-year rolling window, the average 20-day forward return following INDPRO increases is not significantly greater than zero (t-test p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: A rise in the unemployment rate (UNRATE) above 5.5% indicates imminent consumer weakness and potential recessionary dynamics, negatively affecting corporate earnings and stock prices. The signal activates only when the threshold is breached.
failure_condition: In all instances where UNRATE exceeds 5.5% in the test set, the median 20-day SPX return is positive or the hit rate is below 40%.

mechanism_narrative: The simultaneous condition of low federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS < 1%) and low consumer price inflation (CPIAUCSL year-over-year < 2%) creates an accommodative monetary environment that compresses discount rates and supports equity valuations. The signal is derived from the joint state of both series, not from either alone.
failure_condition: When both FEDFUNDS < 1% and CPIAUCSL YoY < 2%, the average 20-day SPX return is not statistically greater than the unconditional average over the same calendar months (Welch t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: In a low inflation environment (CPI YoY < 2%), rising consumer prices reflect healthy demand and pricing power for firms. This regime-conditional mechanism expects a positive relation between CPIAUCSL month-over-month increases and forward equity returns. The direction would flip in a high inflation regime (CPI YoY > 3%).
failure_condition: During low-inflation months identified in the out-of-sample period, the cross-sectional rank correlation between CPIAUCSL monthly change and SPX 60-day forward return is negative or positive but with absolute value < 0.05.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in retail sales (RSAFS) signals weakening consumer demand and worsening economic conditions, which directly hurt corporate revenues and stock prices. The signal is activated only when the month-over-month growth rate turns negative.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS month-over-month growth rate is negative
failure_condition: Over all occurrences of negative RSAFS month-over-month growth, the 5-day forward return for SPX is positive in more than 50% of cases, or the average return is greater than -0.1%.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) are a leading indicator of economic activity; an increase in starts signals strength in construction, employment, and consumer confidence, leading to higher equity prices. The mechanism is always active—any positive reading relative to the prior month is considered bullish.
failure_condition: The average 20-day forward SPX return following months with an increase in HOUST is not significantly positive (one-sided t-test p > 0.10) over any 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: The joint condition of strong GDP growth (GDPC1 quarter-over-quarter annualized > 2%) and low unemployment (UNRATE < 5%) defines a 'sweet spot' for equities where both demand and labor markets are healthy. This interaction creates outsized positive returns as earnings expectations rise and recession risk is minimal.
failure_condition: In quarters when GDPC1 growth > 2% and UNRATE < 5% simultaneously, the subsequent 60-day SPX return is not significantly higher than the return in all other quarters (Mann-Whitney U test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rise in the federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) above 0.75% marks the start of a tightening cycle that increases the discount rate applied to equities and raises borrowing costs, leading to negative forward returns. The signal is triggered only when the rate crosses the 0.75% threshold.
failure_condition: In all instances where FEDFUNDS crosses above 0.75%, the average 20-day SPX return is positive or the ratio of negative returns is less than 55%.
